Recent CAR stories

March 2000
Investigative MethodsData & DatabasesEducation

This collection includes two investigative journalism series: one analyzing high school performance using regression analysis of state test scores and lunch program participation, and another examining deaths of mentally ill and developmentally disabled individuals in North Carolina through death records and police reports.

"Grading Our High Schools" The Pittsburgh Tribune-Review Feb. 6, 2000 http://triblive.com/frames/grades.html

According to the series' author, this investigation "features a regression analysis using average state assessment test scores and participation in the free and reduced-price lunch program."

"Broken Trust" The Charlotte Observer Jan. 23-27, 2000 www.charlotte.com/special/mentalhealth The Observer identified deaths of the mentally ill and developmentally disabled by searching 370,000 N.C. death records from 1994 through mid-1999, along with hundreds of police and autopsy reports.
